Unless I’m mistaken, common versions of ChatGPT and Claude use conversations for training—meaning they can pick up ideas in unpublished work and pass them on to other users; laundering idea plagiarism and creating “scooping” dynamics without any of the people involved knowing it.

Agents will outnumber human users on the web by orders of magnitude. Just like people, they will need a way to pay for services they use. 

They may run into propriety health or finance data they need to pay for when doing a deep research task, or make a tool call to a bespoke

The headline here is just wrong. 

And the key phrase here is “parts of … jobs.”

AI can automate some of the tasks that many people do  (not anywhere 93%)– but (current) AI is wildly uneven and mostly can’t replace full jobs well.

One thing that's interesting in talking to big companies is a lot of them are locked out of AI because their procurement process is too slow.

* Nobody can try any new tools = no bottoms-up growth

* Sales cycles move slowly enough by the time they're buying AI it's old

In the 1990s and 2000s, email “replaced” physical mail and changed the nature of correspondence.

The same thing is happening right now with customer attention. If you think AI is just going to “replace” the call center agent, you’re stuck thinking about first-order effects.

New Gemini API tooling capabilities are here, enabling devs to combine custom function calling and built-in tools (such as Google Search) in a single API call ✨

Start building with:

+ Hybrid tool usage (Built-in + Custom)
+ Cross-tool context circulation
+ Tool response IDs https://t.co/Yzp3SMByFF

Bunch of Gemini API improvements rolled out to improve agentic development 

1. You can ground with Google Maps with Gemini 3
2. Built-in tools (search and url context) work with your custom functions
3. Context circulation
4. Tool response IDs

When LLMs become reliable, you just prompt and ship.
